rationale.summary: The PR replaces first-N log rate limiting with a worker-authoritative random LogSampler and propagates sampling state across worker, RPC, client, logging, deployment, documentation, and test layers. It also changes public/internal logging configuration surfaces, including protobuf fields, EmbeddedConfig APIs, and access-recorder facades. No human transcript was available, so intent alignment cannot be determined from conversation evidence.

keyPoints.point: The change replaces the old rate-limiter model with a worker-authoritative random sampler.
keyPoints.snippet: This PR replaces first-N log rate limiting with a worker-authoritative random LogSampler, propagates sampling state through RPC/client paths

keyPoints.point: The wire format now carries structured sampling config instead of one integer rate limit.
keyPoints.snippet: Register responses now carry request, access, and diagnostic sampling rates in ppm form, and heartbeat responses also gain the same config message

keyPoints.point: The public embedded-worker API changes from a single integer setter to three double sampling-rate setters.
keyPoints.snippet: the public embedded-worker configuration API changes from one integer rate-limit setter to three double sampling-rate setters

keyPoints.point: The PR includes sampler-focused unit, integration, system, access-facade, RPC metadata, and performance coverage.
keyPoints.snippet: The final layer replaces the removed rate-limiter tests with sampler unit, integration, access-facade, RPC metadata, performance, and system-test coverage.

critique_ledger.title: Heartbeat-delivered sampler config has no regression test
critique_ledger.rationale: The new client heartbeat path applies LogSampleConfigPb updates after registration, but the added ST only verifies the initial register-delivered snapshot. If heartbeat responses stop carrying or applying updated sampling config, long-lived clients will keep stale sampler state and this PR's tests would still pass.

critique_ledger.title: New EmbeddedConfig sampler setters are untested
critique_ledger.rationale: The PR adds public embedded-worker configuration setters that silently accept valid rates and ignore invalid, NaN, or out-of-range values, but the diff adds no test that these setters emit the expected request_sample_rate/access_sample_rate/diagnostic_sample_rate args or preserve prior config on invalid input.

critique_ledger.title: Default access logging now allocates and copies an extra formatted request string
critique_ledger.rationale: When the sampler is disabled or all rates fold to pass-through, IsCurrentRequestLogSampled() returns true for every request trace, so LogPerformance() calls FormatAccessReqMsg(), builds a separate ostringstream/string, appends logSampled:true, then copies that result into the outer logStream for every access record.

critique_ledger.title: Repeated protobuf keys are copied into a vector before formatting
critique_ledger.rationale: PROTO_REF access records now allocate a std::vector and copy every protobuf key string before calling objectKeysToString(keys). Worker batch paths such as Exist/DeleteAllCopy/MultiCreate pass RepeatedPtrField values through this path, so sampled-in access logs pay an avoidable O(n) allocation and string-copy cost on batch request handling.

critique_ledger.title: Snapshot publication logic is duplicated across config paths
critique_ledger.rationale: The proto update path manually rebuilds SampleRate objects, allocates a LogSamplerSnapshot, exchanges the atomic pointer, retires the old snapshot, and stores samplerEnabled. The flag path in log_sampler.cpp has a second hand-written version of the same publication sequence.

routed_questions.Unified Random Log Sampling: Do the changed sampler propagation and logging hunks prove that heartbeat-delivered LogSampleConfigPb updates are applied to long-lived clients, while the default access-log path avoids unnecessary allocation/copy overhead when sampling is effectively pass-through?

per_cohort.routed_question: Do the changed sampler propagation and logging hunks prove that heartbeat-delivered LogSampleConfigPb updates are applied to long-lived clients, while the default access-log path avoids unnecessary allocation/copy overhead when sampling is effectively pass-through?

reasons: L4 (Cleanroom) pending for cohort(s): Unified Random Log Sampling
